description.en: Chile's daily bread, invented by French immigrant bakers in Valparaíso: two small dough balls are pressed together with a cross so it looks, and tears, like four rolls in one. A tray of oven steam gives it a glass-crisp, crackling crust.
description.de: Chiles tägliches Brot, erfunden von französischen Einwandererbäckern in Valparaíso: Zwei kleine Teigkugeln werden mit einem Kreuz zusammengedrückt, sodass es wie vier Brötchen in einem aussieht und sich so teilen lässt. Dampf im Ofen sorgt für eine glasig-knusprige Kruste.
description.es: El pan de cada día en Chile, inventado por panaderos inmigrantes franceses en Valparaíso: dos bolitas de masa se presionan con una cruz para que parezca, y se parta, como cuatro panecillos en uno. El vapor en el horno le da una corteza vidriosa y crujiente.
description.fr: Le pain quotidien du Chili, inventé par des boulangers immigrés français à Valparaíso : deux petites boules de pâte sont pressées d'une croix pour qu'il ressemble, et se déchire, comme quatre petits pains en un. La vapeur au four lui donne une croûte vitreuse et craquante.

origin.note: Said to be created by French immigrant brothers named Marraquette in 19th-century Valparaíso; it now accounts for roughly 70% of Chile's daily bread consumption.

yield.shape: two joined 100g balls pressed with a cross, four-lobed look

keeping.note: Best within hours of baking, when the crust still crackles; Chileans buy it fresh nearly every day.

method.title: Activate the yeast
method.summary: Dissolve yeast and a little sugar in warm water and let it stand until foamy.

method.title: Mix the dough
method.summary: Combine flour and salt, then work in the yeasted water until a rough dough forms.

method.title: Knead and ferment
method.summary: Knead about 10 minutes until smooth and uniform, then let it rise until puffy, about 30 minutes.

method.title: Portion and rest
method.summary: Divide into roughly 100g balls and let them rest, covered, for about half an hour.

method.title: Join and mark
method.summary: Press two balls together end to end, then push a floured dowel firmly through the center to create the classic four-lobed silhouette.

method.title: Final rise
method.summary: Let the shaped loaves rest until puffy, then brush lightly with oil.

method.title: Bake with steam
method.summary: Bake in a hot oven with a tray of boiling water for the first stretch, which keeps the crust glassy and lets it expand fully.

source.adaptation: Scaled ratios kept as sourced; 'dry yeast' treated as active-dry yeast.
